name: Camino de la Cava Trail
name_original: Yacimiento arqueológico de La Cava – circular desde Garcinarro
description: Jog a moderate 4.2-mile (6.7 km) circular route from Garcinarro to explore the ancient La Cava Archaeological Site and its rock-carved struc
region_intro: Jogging around Alcarria offers a diverse landscape characterized by undulating plateaus, deep river valleys, and dramatic gorges. The region features limestone and gypsum formations, creating intriguing karst phenomena and scenic canyons carved by rivers like the Tagus. Runners can explore a variety of terrains, from expansive plains to steep slopes, alongside peaceful forests and agricultural lands. Best jogging routes around Alcarria The most popular jogging route is Route of the Faces, a 7.7 miles (12.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 27 minutes to complete. This route combines natural scenery with unique outdoor sculptures near Buendía. Another top favourite among local runners is Sendero del Cucurucho y la Hocecilla – circular trail run from Fuentescusa, a difficult 7.6 miles (12.2 km) path. This route features challenging terrain with significant elevation gain through the region's varied topography. Local runners also love the Virgen del Socorro Trail, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through scenic areas near Sacedón, often completed in about 53 minutes. Jogging around Alcarria is defined by undulating plateaus, river valleys, and distinctive geological formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate jogs to more challenging runs with elevation changes. The routes in Alcarria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 150 runners have used komoot to explore Alcarria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Alcarria?
A: Alcarria offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 35 trails available for you to explore. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes in Alcarria for beginners?
A: Yes, Alcarria has routes suitable for beginners. While most trails are moderate, there is at least one easy route, and many of the moderate trails offer gentle terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014422662/camino-de-la-cava-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de la Cava Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.7 km path with minimal elevation gain, making it a good option for a relaxed run.

Q: Can I find running routes with waterfalls in Alcarria?
A: Yes, Alcarria features scenic running routes that include waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014672043/cascada-campana-del-tio-mil-hombres-running-circular-by-sendero-del-canizar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada Campana del Tío Mil Hombres – running circular by sendero del Cañizar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.6 km circular route that takes you past this beautiful natural feature.

Q: Are there any circular running trails in Alcarria?
A: Absolutely. Many of Alcarria's running routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014672043/cascada-campana-del-tio-mil-hombres-running-circular-by-sendero-del-canizar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada Campana del Tío Mil Hombres&lt;/a&gt; route and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014673257/la-atalaya-running-circular-by-mora-encantada-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Atalaya – running circular by Mora Encantada trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 9.5 km loop.

Q: What are some unique natural features or landmarks I can see while jogging in Alcarria?
A: Alcarria's diverse landscape offers many unique sights. You can jog through the famous lavender fields around Brihuega, especially vibrant in June and July. The region is also known for its river valleys and gorges, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Conde&lt;/a&gt;. Don't miss the distinctive twin hills of Las Tetas de Viana, offering panoramic views, or the intriguing outdoor sculptures along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014429165/route-of-the-faces" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt; near Buendía.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners in Alcarria?
A: Yes, Alcarria provides several challenging routes for experienced runners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014669063/sendero-del-cucurucho-y-la-hocecilla-circular-trail-run-from-fuentescusa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Cucurucho y la Hocecilla – circular trail run from Fuentescusa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 12.2 km path with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31872037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de la Degollada – Molino de la Peña (ruinas) loop from Priego&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 13.8 km route with over 600 meters of ascent.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Alcarria?
A: The running routes in Alcarria are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an impressive average rating of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. Runners frequently praise the varied terrain, from undulating plateaus to scenic river valleys, and the well-maintained paths that offer both exercise and natural beauty.

Q: Can I find running routes with good viewpoints in Alcarria?
A: Alcarria's topography, with its plateaus and river valleys, naturally offers many excellent viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6503495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de los Borrachos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is one such spot where you can enjoy expansive vistas. Routes around landmarks like Las Tetas de Viana also provide panoramic views of the region.

Q: Are there any running routes near reservoirs in Alcarria?
A: Yes, the region features beautiful reservoirs that offer tranquil settings for running. The Pantano de Entrepeñas (Entrepeñas Reservoir) is a prominent example, surrounded by characteristic Alcarria vegetation, providing scenic routes alongside the water.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Alcarria?
A: The best time for jogging in Alcarria is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be pleasant, especially in the early mornings or late evenings, and is particularly special if you want to experience the blooming lavender fields around Brihuega in June and July. Winter offers a different, often quieter, experience, but be prepared for cooler temperatures.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the running trails?
A: Yes, Alcarria is rich in history and culture. The famous 'Viaje a la Alcarria' route, inspired by Camilo José Cela's book, passes through many historic towns and natural areas. You can also find routes that offer views of historic structures, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31507374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Alcocer&lt;/a&gt;, which explores areas with local heritage.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly scenic or picturesque?
A: Many routes in Alcarria are incredibly scenic. The 'Jardín de La Alcarria' near Brihuega, especially during lavender season, is exceptionally picturesque. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031416599/virgen-del-socorro-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen del Socorro Trail&lt;/a&gt; near Sacedón also leads through beautiful areas, offering a blend of natural beauty and serene landscapes.
tips: La Cava is a 12-hectare multiphase archaeological site that was first occupied 4,000 years ago, during the Bronze Age (between 2,500 and 1,000 BC). It is located on top of a hill whose western front is almost inaccessible, with the Sierra de Altomira in the background.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Archaeological site of La Cava – circular from Garcinarro route?
faqs.answer: While specific details for jogging are limited, the access paths to the archaeological site are generally described as dirt tracks. Expect varied surfaces typical of historical sites and rural trails, potentially including uneven ground and rocky sections, especially around the ancient structures.

faqs.question: Is the route suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: The route is graded as 'moderate' with a relatively low elevation gain of about 50 meters over 6.7 kilometers. This suggests it's manageable for those with some jogging experience, but beginners should be prepared for the moderate difficulty and potential uneven terrain around the archaeological site.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary point of interest is the La Cava Archaeological Site itself. This ancient Iberian and Celtiberian settlement features rupestrian buildings from the Iron Age and rock-carved structures, including ancient 'cups'. The site is strategically located on a hill, offering wide views of the surrounding area, including the Sierra de Altomira.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the La Cava Archaeological Site is situated on a hill, providing expansive views of the area, including the Sierra de Altomira. These panoramic vistas are a significant feature of the route.

faqs.question: Where can I park near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact jogging route are not detailed, access paths to the Archaeological site of La Cava are mentioned as being signposted and maintained. It's advisable to look for designated parking in or near Garcinarro, or at the main access points leading to the archaeological site.

faqs.question: Is the Archaeological site of La Cava – circular from Garcinarro route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness for this specific jogging route is not explicitly available. When visiting archaeological sites, it's generally best to check local regulations regarding pets, especially concerning leash rules and access to historical structures. Always ensure your dog is under control and clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this route would generally be during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. These seasons offer pleasant weather for outdoor activities and can enhance the experience of exploring the archaeological site and enjoying the views without the extreme heat of summer or potential chill of winter.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the archaeological site or the trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to access the Archaeological site of La Cava or the surrounding trails. Access paths are described as maintained for general visitor circulation, suggesting open access. However, it's always good practice to check for any local updates before your visit.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog the Archaeological site of La Cava – circular from Garcinarro route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 6.7 kilometers long and typically takes about 52 minutes to jog, depending on your pace and how much time you spend exploring the archaeological site.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 10% of this route follows a section of the official trail known as Camino de La Cava.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trail?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify facilities directly on the trail or at the archaeological site itself. For cafes, pubs, or restrooms, you would likely need to visit the nearby village of Garcinarro before or after your jog.
